subject: Some issues with tilt series processing
body:
Hi Dimitry,

I am trying to process some older tilt series data through warp, and
running into an interesting error. The data was collected at a period of
time when we were saving frames into individual folders for each tilt
series, so in order to better match the workflow of warp I moved a couple
of tilt series frames to a single folder (out of their subfolders from
serialEM). I did the same for the mdoc files for the whole tilt series. I
am able to do frame processing, export to imod and perform alignment in
etomo, but when trying to bring the alignment files back into warp to make
tomostars, I get a pair of the following errors that prevent import:
"Object reference not set to an instance of an object."

Is this because I moved the files around in a non-ideal way? Do I need to
preserve the original relative folder structure? Since warp succeeded in
everything up to and including exporting to etomo, I am a little surprised
to run into these errors this far down the line. Another possible issue is
that we have recalibrated the pixel size since these were collected, and I
manually updated the pixel size during frame alignment - but again, I would
have expected the issue to arise when the rawtlt and st files were made out
of the frames and mdoc files.

I should add that I've gotten newer data (collected to match the folder
structure of EMD-10164) through the pipeline successfully, which is why I
suspect the issue is one of these two differences with the old data.

Benjamin Barad, PhD
Postdoctoral Associate
Grotjahn Lab @ Scripps Research
He/His/Him
